spec_match.rationale: The dashboard remains an admin fleet simulator for the Chrome 151 macOS 13 minimum and now supports repeat use through persisted model state.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified range updates, localStorage save/reset, CSV export status, and live runway calculations.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page delivers the fleet-size, distribution, and refresh-velocity planning promised by the feature index.

edge_cases.rationale: The previous persistence/export gap is covered with Save model, Reset saved, Download CSV, and an aria-live status.

design_system.rationale: The new controls use CSS variables, associated labels, and explicit button color/background pairs.

summary: Re-tested in Chrome Canary through chrome-devtools-mcp at /v151/chrome-will-remove-support-for-macos-12/fleet-readiness-dashboard/: changing Monterey to 33 saved that model to localStorage, Download CSV updated the status to CSV exported, Reset saved cleared the model, and console/network output was clean.
